name: Trump
why_line: The House pushes State Department funding through 217-209 on party lines — Trump's Iran and defense priorities clear a chamber, a partisan win but a single-chamber one, still short of law.

name: Musk
why_line: The trillionaire trade keeps unwinding — SpaceX sits below its IPO price with an aborted Starship flight on the same tape, and the $8.7B short pile is the market telling Musk the meme ascent is running in reverse.

name: Warsh
why_line: Warsh keeps remaking the Fed in his image — new task forces, new leadership, the structural reorganization of monetary-policy conduct now bearing his fingerprints.

name: Altman
why_line: Apple drags Altman into court on trade-secret theft — the fresh TechCrunch filing detail lands squarely on OpenAI's IPO runway, exactly the litigation overhang no S-1 wants.

name: Amodei
why_line: After the gulag reports and the timeline mea culpa, Zuckerberg finally ships something — Muse Spark 1.1 crashes the enterprise coding fight, another pricing threat to Amodei.

name: Ferguson
why_line: Ferguson lands his second drug-middleman scalp with a Caremark settlement — the FTC's PBM campaign is now a pattern, not a one-off, and the 'as-a-service' lock-in crowd should be watching.

name: Abel
why_line: Berkshire's new hand: tripled the Alphabet stake then deployed $16.8B more - the quietest capital in America just picked a side in the AI war.

name: Huang
why_line: Washington quietly reclassifies the UAE into the friendly column and reopens the Gulf as an AI-chip market — Huang gets a whole new export lane written into the Federal Register.

name: Dimon
why_line: Dimon's machine runs toward a $1T market cap on record fees and trading windfalls, and his $280M retention award is the board pricing exactly how irreplaceable it thinks he is.

name: Bessent
why_line: Bessent's Economic Fury campaign keeps grinding — a fresh OFAC wave on Iran's shadow shipping fleet, timed to the blockade, the Treasury enforcer doing what he sells to the G7.

name: Griffin
why_line: Griffin wires $400M into Crypto.com at a $20B mark — Wall Street's most openly political operator plants a flag in the institutional crypto infrastructure buildout.

name: Andreessen
why_line: a16z's capture machine gets a Pentagon badge — Andreessen wires himself into defense policy directly, turning the Valley-DC pipeline into a formal seat at the table.

name: Jassy
why_line: Jassy drops a billion on a Forward Deployed Engineering arm — Amazon copying the OpenAI-Anthropic enterprise playbook to force AWS into every boardroom deployment.

name: Powell
why_line: Handed Warsh the chair but kept his Board seat - the in-house dissident with two years left and a warning shot already fired.

name: Bezos
why_line: Back in an operator's seat for the first time since Amazon - Prometheus at $41B with JPMorgan and BlackRock money, and Musk already calls him a copycat.

name: Fink
why_line: HPS put BlackRock's $14T machine deep inside the private credit stress - and his money is quietly inside Bezos's Prometheus too.

name: Hassabis
why_line: DeepMind's models power Gemini's run at ChatGPT's market share - the lab quietly winning the frontier race from inside Google.

name: Solomon
why_line: #1 in M&A with record revenues - Goldman's deal machine sits at the toll booth of the IPO wave Musk, Altman, and Amodei are about to ride.

name: Rowan
why_line: Gated his flagship credit fund at 45 cents on the requested dollar and called peers idiots - the loudest voice insisting the private credit fire is containable.

name: Atkins
why_line: The SEC's posture on crypto and the AI IPO wave runs through his desk - including whether Musk's consent order gets rolled back.

name: Schwarzman
why_line: Blackstone loosened its gates while Apollo held firm - a public bet that confidence beats caution in the credit stress.

name: Gray
why_line: The Senate moves 87-8 to wall institutional buyers out of single-family homes — a Washington supermajority aimed straight at the Blackstone playbook, and Gray's rental empire is the named target.

name: Nadella
why_line: The vibe-coding thesis reaches the Fortune 500 — Starbucks is building in-house to dump Microsoft and IBM enterprise software, and Nadella's seat-license moat starts to crack from the demand side.

name: Warren
why_line: SCOTUS hands Trump the FTC's neck while sparing the Fed — the firewall that made independent agencies independent is gutted, and Warren's model of insulated regulators takes the constitutional hit.

name: Karp
why_line: Karp admits his own government customers are walking to open-source — after France's DGSI dropped Palantir, the second crack in the proprietary-lock-in thesis comes from Karp's own mouth.

name: Zuckerberg
why_line: Zuckerberg yanks another AI feature within days of launch and now faces a discrimination suit over using AI to run layoffs — the Meta AI unit's rough streak keeps compounding in public.

name: Pichai
why_line: The Chrome divestiture threat won't die — DOJ's remedies hearing grinds forward on the confirmed liability finding, and Pichai's structural nightmare stays live while his lab bleeds talent.

name: Blanche
why_line: A judge finds the court was misused in the IRS fight and refers lawyers for discipline just as Blanche's confirmation runs into bipartisan heat over an audit-shielding settlement — the shadow AG walks into his hearing already bleeding.

name: Pulte
why_line: Acting DNI amid bipartisan fury - chaos generates events.

name: Krishnan
why_line: Departed WH AI advisor - watch where he lands.

name: Massie
why_line: Led the war-powers rebuke that cost the President 8 points.

name: Ellison
why_line: Oracle's AI spending blew past estimates and the credit market flinched - the debt bill on the buildout coming due in public.

name: Hegseth
why_line: Hegseth brags the Pentagon kicked Anthropic out 'forever' three months ago — and Mark Kelly's backing makes the pile-on bipartisan, the signal Amodei should fear most.

name: Thune
why_line: Thune strips Trump's voter-ID demand off the FISA bill and dares him to fight it — the Senate leader publicly choosing his own chamber over the President's wish list.

name: Cook
why_line: SCOTUS splits the baby and Trump still wins the war — Cook keeps her chair but the Court hands the President the FTC's neck, gutting the firewall that made independent agencies independent.

name: Tan
why_line: Tan books Apple through 2031 — Broadcom quietly becomes the silicon spine of Cupertino, the same co-signer Anthropic and OpenAI already lean on for their custom chips.

name: Graham
why_line: Graham is gone — the aortic dissection took the Senate's chief Russia-sanctions architect and its loudest defense hawk overnight, leaving a hole in the hawkish coalition Trump's war footing depends on.

name: McConnell
why_line: McConnell concedes he can't come back to vote — three weeks in the hospital becomes a structural problem when the Defense Appropriations chair's empty chair puts Trump's $1.5T defense budget one vote short.

name: Khosla
why_line: Khosla plants a $9.6B flag on an NFL franchise — the largest single-asset buy any Valley VC has ever made, the clearest sign yet that AI-era paper is converting into hard trophy assets.

name: Krishna
why_line: IBM's worst single day ever hangs on Krishna — enterprise clients yanked spend from legacy software to AI hardware, and the structural reallocation everyone theorized just showed up on one CEO's tape.

name: Collison (Patrick & John)
why_line: The Collisons put $53.4B on the table for PayPal — Stripe stops eating share quietly and moves to swallow the incumbent whole, a bid, not a close, but the ambition is now on the record.

name: Amodei (Dario)
why_line: [new - pending editorial why-line]

name: Nadella (Satya)
why_line: Nadella turns his own sales force against his biggest partner — Microsoft reps now coached to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic on price, the frenemy mask slipping in Altman's direction just as the IPO clock runs.

name: Altman (Sam)
why_line: Nadella turns his own sales force against his biggest partner — Microsoft reps now coached to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic on price, the frenemy mask slipping in Altman's direction just as the IPO clock runs.

name: Cook (Tim)
why_line: Cook clears the regulatory gate for Apple Intelligence in China on an Alibaba/Baidu stack — the toughest market unlocked at the cost of a geopolitically bifurcated AI, a fresh development past the earlier Qwen approval.

name: Musk (Elon)
why_line: The trillionaire trade unwinds — SpaceX breaks below its $135 IPO price for the first time after four red sessions, and the meme-stock ascent that minted Musk's paper wealth is now running in reverse.

name: Tan (Hock)
why_line: Tan books Apple through 2031 — Broadcom becomes the silicon spine of Cupertino, the same co-signer Anthropic and OpenAI already lean on for custom chips.

name: Huang (Jensen)
why_line: [new - pending editorial why-line]

name: Bessent (Scott)
why_line: Bessent's Economic Fury grinds on — a fresh OFAC wave on Iran's weapons-procurement web and Shamkhani's shadow fleet, timed to the blockade, the Treasury enforcer doing what he sells to the G7.

name: Massie (Thomas)
why_line: Massie splits the Democratic caucus down the middle on Israel aid — 100 Democrats cross over, and even a failed amendment becomes the story because the rebuke itself is the seismic shift.

name: Warsh (Kevin)
why_line: [new - pending editorial why-line]

name: Zuckerberg (Mark)
why_line: [new - pending editorial why-line]
